chunk_summary: Guidelines for prescribing and safely using RUSF and LNS for moderate wasting, including dosing, administration, and storage recommendations, with emphasis on preventing misuse and ensuring adherence.
learning_objectives: Understand the appropriate dosing and energy contributions of RUSF and LNS for children with moderate wasting.
Learn safe-use practices for RUSF, including storage, administration, and prevention of food displacement or sharing.
Recognize the differences between RUSF, RUTF, and other lipid-based nutrient supplements in terms of intent and application.
key_facts: WHO recovery target: 100–130 kcal/kg/day total energy needs, with supplements contributing 40–60% (40–78 kcal/kg/day).
Weight-banded energy targets: 6 kg child needs 240–468 kcal/day; 8 kg child needs 320–624 kcal/day; 10 kg child needs 400–780 kcal/day from supplements.
RUSF specifications: 1 sachet (100 g) provides ~535 kcal, eaten directly without cooking or dilution.
Prescribed course duration: Typically 8–12 weeks, with early reassessment for adherence and displacement.
Safe-use practices: Store sealed, avoid mixing with water, supervise young children to prevent choking, and screen for food allergies.
reference_quote: UNICEF specifications describe RUSF as eaten directly from the sachet without cooking or dilution, typically as one 100 g sachet per day providing about 535 kcal, and not as a breast-milk substitute.
document_key_topics: Prescribable dosing of RUSF and LNS
